title: Container transfer device
abstract: The invention relates to a device, system and method for supporting multiple sized storage containers in an automated storage and retrieval system. The automated storage system employs a container transfer device for supporting and transferring multiple sized storage containers to an access opening of the workstation outside the framework structure. The container transfer device is provided with one or more supports (carrier) that support multiple sized containers, at least one mechanism for moving the support and presenting the support to a presenting position at the workstation. The container transfer system is provided with container stabilizing devices within the support that enable the support to hold and support multiple sized storage containers during movement. The container stabilizing elements ensure stability to the storage containers and allow flexibility in terms of size of the containers to be supported within the support.
claims:
Claims 1. A container transfer device (610) for moving storage containers (106) between a port (119, 120) and an access opening (602) of a workstation (600) wherein the device (610) comprises: - a support (620) for supporting one or more storage containers (106) during movement of the storage containers (106); and - a mechanism (640) for moving the support (620) between the port (119, 120) and the access opening (602), wherein the support (620) comprises: - a side wall (622) for restraining the one or more storage containers (106) on the support (620) during movement of the support (620); and - one or more container stabilizing device (626) arranged within the at least one side wall (622) to stabilize the one or more storage containers (106). 2. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 1, wherein the support (620) comprises a base (630) for supporting the one or more storage containers (106). 3. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 1 or 2, wherein the device (610) comprises a plurality of supports (620) for supporting the one or more storage containers (106). 4. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with any one of the preceding claims, wherein at least one of the container stabilizing device (626) is displaceable between a stowed position for when the support (620) is being used to move a large storage container (10) and a stabilizing position for when the support (620) is being used to move a smaller storage container (20), the container stabilizing device (626) being arranged to work with the side wall (622) and/or the base (630) of the support (620) to stabilize the smaller container (20) during movement of the support (620). 5. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with any one of the preceding claims, wherein the support (620) comprises an upper rim (624) for preventing movements of the one or more storage containers (106) during movement of the support (620); and wherein the upper rim (624) comprises protrusions (625) arranged on an inner surface of the upper rim (624) and wherein the protrusions (625) are shaped to allow the one or more storage containers (106) to be supported thereon. 6. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with any one of the preceding claims, wherein the one or more container stabilizing device (626) is a longitudinal guiding element (628) arranged at a center of each inner side wall (622); and 

 wherein the longitudinal guiding element (628) is configured to lock the one or more storage containers (106) in place on the support (620). 7. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 6, wherein the longitudinal guiding element (628) comprises at least one groove extending along a length of the longitudinal guiding element (628). 8. The container transfer device (610) in accordance claim 6 or 7, wherein an additional longitudinal guiding element (627) extends from a center point of the support (620) parallel to the side wall (622), wherein the additional longitudinal guiding element (627) is configured to fit into corresponding complementary shaped recesses (106â, 106â) of the one or more storage containers (106). 9. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 8, wherein the additional longitudinal guiding element (627) has a cross shaped profile. 10. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with any one of the claims 1 to 5, wherein the one or more container stabilizing device (626) extends from an inner side wall (622) or the base (630) of the support (620). 11. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 10, wherein the one or more of container stabilizing devices (626) are configured to fit into corresponding complementary shaped recesses (106â, 106â) of the one or more storage containers (106). 12. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 10, wherein at least one of the one or more container stabilizing devices (626) is a base flap (629a) arranged on a base (630) of the support (620), wherein the base flap (629a) is configured to be displaced from the stowed position parallel to the base (630) to the stabilizing position perpendicular to the base (630). 13. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claims 10 to 11, wherein at least one of the one or more container stabilizing devices (626) is a side flap (629b) provided on the inner surface of the at least one side wall (622), the side flap (629b) is configured to be displaced from the stowed position parallel to the side wall (622), to open inwards towards a center of the support (620) to the stabilizing position. 14. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claims 10 to 11, wherein the support (620) comprises an actuation device (631) configured to deploy one or more of the container stabilizing devices (626) for stabilizing the smaller container (20) on the support (620). 15. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 14, wherein the actuation device (631) is configured to deploy the one or more of the container stabilizing devices (626) based on an arrangement of the one or more storage containers (106) to be placed within the support (620). 16. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with any one of the preceding claims, wherein the mechanism (640) for moving the support (620) comprises: an axle assembly (642), at least one motor that operates the axle assembly (642) and one or more arms (646) connecting the support (620) to the axle assembly (640) and wherein the mechanism (640) moves the support (620) between the port (119) and the access opening (602). 17. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 16, wherein the mechanism (640) for moving the support (620) is configured to generate a carousel movement in relation to the access opening (602). 18. The container transfer device (610) in accordance with claim 16, wherein the mechanism (640) is configured for moving the support (620) in a linear trajectory to displace the support (620) between the port (119) and the access opening (602). 19. An automated storage and retrieval system (1) comprising - a container transfer device (610) in accordance with claims 1 to 18; - wherein the workstation (600) is suitable for accessing storage containers (106) received from the port (119, 120); - a framework structure (100) for storing the one or more storage containers (106) in stacks (107), the framework structure (100) comprising a rail system (108) arranged above the stacks (107), the rail system (108) comprising perpendicular rails (110, 111), the intersections of which form a horizontal grid structure having grid cells (112) defining grid openings; a plurality of vertical upright members (102) defining a plurality of storage columns (105) for storing the stacks (107); and a port column (119,120) for transferring a storage container (106) between the rail system (108) and the workstation (600) positioned outside the framework structure (100), wherein the port column (119, 120) is arranged at least partly above the container transfer device (610); - a plurality of container handling vehicles (200, 300, 400) configured to travel along the rail system (108) and to transfer storage containers (106) through the port column (119, 120) to the container transfer device (610); and - a control system (109) configured to send instructions to control operations of at least one of the container handling vehicles (200, 300, 400) and the container transfer device (610). 20. The automated storage and retrieval system (1) in accordance with claim 19, wherein each of the container handling vehicles (200,300,400) comprises a plurality of gripper devices (304a-d), wherein the one or more gripper devices (304a-d) are selected for gripping one or more of the containers (106) based on the desired distribution of storage containers (106) to be transferred to the support (620). 21. The automated storage and retrieval system (1) in accordance with claim 19 or 20, wherein the support (620) of the container transfer device (610) is positioned relative to the port column (119) such that the container handling vehicles (200,300,400) may release the one or more storage containers (106) onto the support (620). 22. The automated storage and retrieval system (1) in accordance with any one of claims 19 to 21, wherein the workstation (600) is arranged to at least partly enclose the container transfer device (610), wherein the workstation (600) provides access to the contents of the container transfer device (610) through the access opening (602). 23. The automated storage and retrieval system (1) in accordance with claim 22, wherein the access opening (602) is provided at an upper surface of the workstation (600) and comprises a slidable cover (603) arranged to provide an operator access to contents within the support (610). 24. A method for transferring one or more storage containers (106) between a workstation (600) and the port (119, 120) within an automated storage and retrieval system (1) in accordance with any one of claims 19 to 23, wherein the method comprises the following steps: - sending instruction signals to the container transfer device (610) containing information regarding a number and size of the storage containers (106) that is transferred to the support (620) of the container transfer device (610); - activating the one or more container stabilizing device (626) provided on an inner surface of the support (620); - moving the support (620) away from the access opening (602) to position the support (620) below the port column (119,120); - activating one or more gripper devices (304a-d) of the container handling vehicle (200,300,400) depending on the number and size of 

 the one or more storage containers (106) supported by the support (620); and - transferring the one or more storage containers (106) by the container handling vehicle (200,300,600) through the port column (119,120) to the storage volume (104). 25. The method in accordance with claim 24, wherein at least one of the one or more container stabilizing device is a base flap (629a) arranged on a base (630) of the support (620) and is configured to be displaced from a stowed position parallel to the base (630) to a stabilizing position perpendicular to the base (630); wherein the container stabilizing device (626) comprises - a first actuation device (632a) for activating said base flap (629a); and wherein the method further comprises the step of: - rotating the base flap (629a) from a closed position parallel to a base of the support (620) to an open position perpendicular to the base of the support (620) when activated by the first actuation device (632a). 26. The method in accordance with claim 24 or 25, wherein at least one of the one or more container stabilizing device is a side flap (629b) positioned on an inner surface of the side wall (622) of the support (620) and which is configured to open inwards towards a center of the support (620) to a stabilizing position; wherein the container stabilizing device (626) comprises - a second actuation device (632b) for activating said side flap (629b); and wherein the method further comprises the step of - rotating the side flap (629b) from a closed position parallel to the side wall (622) to an open position perpendicular to the side wall (622) towards a center of the support (620) when activated by the second actuation device (632b). 27. The method in accordance with any one of the preceding claims 24 to 26, wherein the method comprises the steps of - moving the support (620) in a carousel movement relative to the access opening (602); and - positioning the support (620) directly below the port column (119,120) for subsequent retrieval by the container handling vehicle (200, 300, 400). 28. The method in accordance with any one of the preceding claims 24 to 26, wherein the method comprises the steps of: - moving the support (620) in a linear trajectory motion relative to the access opening (602); and - positioning the support (620) directly below the port column (119,120) for subsequent retrieval by the container handling vehicle (200,300,400). 29. A computer program product comprising a controller in communication with the control system (109) and wherein the control system (109) is configured to carry out transfer of storage containers (106) between a workstation (600) and a storage volume (104) within the automated storage and retrieval system (1) and comprises instructions which, when the program is executed by a computer, cause the computer to carry out the method of claims 24 to 28.
